text.title: Threshold-Based Tool-Result Eviction
text.summary: Any single tool result over N tokens is rewritten to disk by middleware before the LLM sees it; the message becomes a path plus short preview.

section.title: The rule
section.p: The runtime — not the model — decides whether a tool result enters context. A token counter sits between the tool and the message history; oversized results are persisted to a scratch directory and the message is replaced with a tiny manifest.

rule.statement: Configure the agent runtime so any tool result whose token count exceeds a fixed threshold N (default 20,000) is written to disk at a unique path and substituted with a JSON object containing at minimum {path, preview_first_n_chars, total_tokens, evicted: true} before being added to the message history.
rule.rationale: LLMs cannot reliably self-truncate large outputs; LangChain Deep Agents shipped this exact policy (`toolTokenLimitBeforeEvict`, default 20k) after observing context corruption from oversized tool returns on long-horizon agents.

example.code: # Tool returns 35k tokens of npm install log # Middleware detects size > 20k, writes /tmp/agent/logs/npm_3491.log # Message added to history: { "tool": "shell", "result": { "path": "/tmp/agent/logs/npm_3491.log", "preview": "added 1247 packages, audited 1248 packages in 47s\n12 vulnerabilities (3 moderate...", "total_tokens": 34812, "evicted": true } } # Agent reads only what it needs: read_file("/tmp/agent/logs/npm_3491.log", lines=[1200, 1260]) # the lines with the error
example.note: Context grows by ~80 tokens instead of 34,812; the body is one Read away if needed.
One overflow corrupts the rest of the loop because effective context is dominated by noise.

antipattern.description: Asking the LLM, in the system prompt, to "summarize tool outputs longer than 20k tokens before continuing".
antipattern.reason: The model has already seen the 35k tokens by the time it can summarize — context damage is done. Eviction must happen before the message reaches the model.

section.reference: LangChain Deep Agents filesystem middleware — the canonical implementation.
LangChain — Context Management for Deep Agents (rationale and `toolTokenLimitBeforeEvict` knob).
